channel.title: Modulus on Negative Numbers on My New Hugo Site
channel.description: Recent content in Modulus on Negative Numbers on My New Hugo Site

item.title: Day 2: Gift Shop
item.description: This is my solution to https://adventofcode.com/2025/day/2. Part 1 The Gist Given a list of comma-separated number ranges, scan for invalid IDs formed by a sequence of digits repeated exactly twice (such as 55 or 123123) with no leading zeroes, and calculate the total sum of these numbers. Slicing numbers without string manipulation Using pure math is better than string manipulation because… Math happens in the stack, string manipulation needs memory on the heap.

item.title: Day 3: Lobby
item.description: This is my solution to https://adventofcode.com/2025/day/3. Part 1 The Gist Given a list of battery banks represented as strings of digits, identify the two digits within each bank that form the highest possible numerical value while maintaining their original relative order, then calculate the sum of these maximum joltages across all banks. Code for part 1 package main import ( "bufio" "fmt" "os" ) type BatteryRow struct { Readings []int } func main() { data, err := ParseDataFromFile("input.

item.title: Day 1, Secret Entrance
item.description: This is my solution to https://adventofcode.com/2025/day/1. Part 1 The Gist Given a list of instructions for turning a 100 position circular safe dial (numbered 0 to 99), track the position of the dial as we spin it left and right, and count how many times it lands on exactly 0. Notes on Modulus on Negative Numbers In Go, % doesn’t mean modulo, it means remainder. Because of this, it cannot be used directly to find the modulus of negative numbers.
